calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate
Off Calendar Aug-12-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. Abenicio Cisneros, Esq. of Law Office of Abenicio Cisneros (tel: 707-653-0438) for the Petitioner.
Atty. Sarah Johansen, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP (tel: 415-543-8700) for the Respondent.

Hearing Re: PETITIONER PARTNERSHIP FOR CIVIL JUSTICE FUND's Motion To Bifurcate.

Disclosure: Judge Van Aken discloses that her spouse is employed as a tenured professor at a UC campus. He has no connection to the events of this case other than his employment. Disqualification is not required in this circumstance and the court concludes that a reasonable person aware of the facts would not reasonably entertain a doubt that the judge would be able to be impartial. (See Rothman, California Judicial Conduct Handbook (4th ed. 2017) 7:46 at pp. 464-465; CCP 170.1(a)(6)(A)(iii).)

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR# 5229 (tel: 415-828-1919 / email: peggytsujimoto@gmail.com) (301/CVA)

***** After the hearing, the Court signs an order denying Petitioner's motion to bifurcate and setting production date (see signed order for complete ruling). *****

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Of Prompt Production Under The California Public Records Act
Off Calendar Aug-12-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. Abenicio Cisneros, Esq. of Law Office of Abenicio Cisneros (tel: 707-653-0438) for the Petitioner.
Atty. Sarah Johansen, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP (tel: 415-543-8700) for the Respondent.

Hearing Re: PETITIONER PARTNERSHIP FOR CIVIL JUSTICE FUND's Motion For An Order Of Prompt Production Under The California Public Records Act.

Disclosure: Judge Van Aken discloses that her spouse is employed as a tenured professor at a UC campus. He has no connection to the events of this case other than his employment. Disqualification is not required in this circumstance and the court concludes that a reasonable person aware of the facts would not reasonably entertain a doubt that the judge would be able to be impartial. (See Rothman, California Judicial Conduct Handbook (4th ed. 2017) 7:46 at pp. 464-465; CCP 170.1(a)(6)(A)(iii).)

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR# 5229 (tel: 415-828-1919 / email: peggytsujimoto@gmail.com) (301/CVA)

***** After the hearing, the Court signs an order denying petitioner's motion of prompt production (see signed order for complete ruling).*****
